title: diholcos jepsonii
intro: Diholcos jepsonii, also known as California milk vetch, is a perennial legume native to California and a member of the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, coughs, and rheumatism due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Consuming the plant or its extracts may provide health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, improving digestion, and reducing stress. Diholcos jepsonii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
